description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player with a large, flared brass horn mounted on a decorative, dark wooden base. The base features ornate metallic inlays and carved patterns, giving it an antique appearance. A circular turntable platter sits atop the base, holding a black vinyl record with a purple label at its center. The tonearm, made of metal, is positioned over the record and is connected to a visible crank handle on the side. The horn is attached to the tonearm assembly and curves upward, dominating the upper portion of the object. The overall design is both functional and highly decorative, reminiscent of early 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden base, and visible turntable platter holding a vinyl record.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved brass horn, ornate wooden base, and the crank handle on the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide opening of the brass horn, decorative front panel, and centered turntable platter.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn extending left, crank handle on the left, and clear view of the tonearm over the record.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the horn, the tonearm's attachment, and the plain back panel of the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and circular, occupying the central top area of the base. It appears to be made of black material, likely metal or hard plastic, with a felt or rubber mat on top. A vinyl record with a purple label is placed on it. The platter is smooth, with no visible grooves or patterns except for the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and moderately long, extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is slightly curved and features a visible joint near its base, allowing it to pivot. The finish is metallic, matching the horn's aesthetic, and it is currently positioned over the record.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
attribute_control_knobs: A single crank handle is visible on the side of the base, made of metal with a black grip. No other control knobs, switches, or buttons are visible.
